commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Searching into what time, or manner of time. The ancient prophets with longing and ardent desires, obtained to know of the Holy Spirit, the spirit of Christ, the time and the glory that followed those sufferings, by Christ's resurrection and ascension. All these were revealed to them, and they saw that they ministered things to you, not to themselves; that is, that these things they were ministers of, in prophesying about them, were not to happen in their time, but are not come to pass, as they have been preached to you. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Let none think it was with some other, because no other exists, except that of Christ subsequently; which at that time, of course, could not be given by His disciples, inasmuch as the glory of the Lord had not yet been fully attained,
